title: Measure — details

fields.label: Age (min)
fields.name: age_min
fields.instructions: The youngest age (in years) for which the measure has been validated for

fields.label: Age (max)
fields.name: age_max
fields.instructions: The oldest age (in years) for which the measure has been validated for

fields.label: Age Other
fields.name: age_description
fields.instructions: Please add any other age-related information (if applicable)

fields.label: Time
fields.name: time
fields.instructions: State how long (in minutes) it is likely to take a respondent to complete the measure. Please record this in multiples of 5 (e.g., 5 minutes, 10 minutes, 15 minutes etc.)

fields.label: Respondent
fields.name: respondent
fields.instructions: Who will be asked to complete the measure?  Please select from the options below:

fields.label: Problem areas
fields.name: problem_areas
fields.instructions: Using the options below, please select relevant problem areas that the measure may be applicable to.

fields.label: Keywords
fields.name: keywords
fields.instructions: Please enter a comma-separated series of key words that can make it easier to search for and find this measure.

fields.label: Summary
fields.name: summary
fields.instructions:
Write a short summary of the measure
<cite>The Child Avoidance Measure – Parent report (CAMP) is an 8-Item parent-report measure of a child’s avoidance behaviours and is designed for children aged 7 to 18 years of age (Whiteside et al., 2013). The CAMP items include reference to a range of avoidance behaviours which are combined to provide a single overall measure of a child’s tendency to avoid 

anxiety eliciting stimuli. Reflecting the transdiagnostic nature of avoidance in anxiety disorders, the CAMP is designed to be applicable across anxiety disorders.</cite>

fields.label: Additional information
fields.name: overview
fields.instructions: Please include any other details you feel potential users may find useful. This may be other versions of the measure, clinical application, diagnostic relevance, language translations.

fields.label: Scoring
fields.name: scoring
fields.instructions:
Scoring: Include details of how to score the questionnaire, including any formulas and reverse- scored items. If recommendations for classification of high/low scores or normed data are available, this can also be provided.
<cite>The Child Avoidance Measure – Parent report (CAMP) is a brief measure of avoidance behaviour in children and young people. The questionnaire is completed by a parent/carer. 

Parent/Carers are asked to respond to eight statements on how their children react to things that make them scared or worried. All items are preceded by the general stem statement: 

“When I/my child feel(s) scared or worried about something ...” and measures three aspects of behavioural avoidance:  passive avoidance (e.g., “...s/he tries to avoid it”), active avoidance (e.g., “...s/he tries to get away from it”), and refusal (e.g., “...s/he asks me to take care of it for him/her”). Responses are scored on a 4-point Likert scale ranging from 0 (never) to 3 (almost always). Total scores are a sum of all 8 items and scores range from 0 to 24. Higher scores indicate higher levels of behavioural avoidance behaviours.</cite>
